name: Ulmug gro-Cromgog
bio:
Physical Appearance: Orsimer (Orc) male. Wears an almost complete set of heavy armor including cuirass, boots, gauntlets, and greaves - all of the best quality. Armed with a battle axe of similar quality. Battle-ready equipment befitting a professional mercenary.
Race: Orsimer
Personality Keywords: vigilant, dedicated, mercenary, hostile, protective, tireless
Role: Ulmug gro-Cromgog is a warrior and mercenary currently employed to guard Roderick, who lies near death in Fort Sutch northeast of Anvil. He and his fellow mercenaries don't know that the Dark Brotherhood has been hired to eliminate Roderick, making their protection efforts ultimately futile.
Location Information: Fort Sutch, where he patrols the northeastern section constantly. He remains there indefinitely even after Roderick's fate is sealed.
Daily Routine: Ulmug maintains a tireless vigil, patrolling twenty-four hours a day without eating or sleeping. His movements are somewhat random but he mostly sticks to the northern walls, taking only occasional strolls around the dining area in the middle of the fort. He represents the final obstacle before reaching the medicine cabinet where Roderick's vital medicine can be accessed.
Equipment: Ulmug carries the gate key to Fort Sutch and a small amount of gold, along with his high-quality armor and battle axe.
The Conversation: When someone first enters the fort, Ulmug can be overheard conversing with fellow mercenary Neesha in the big central room. Afterwards, they accompany each other toward Roderick's sickbed before splitting up to resume their patrols.
Hostile Nature: Ulmug and the other mercenaries attack intruders on sight, taking their protective duties seriously. They're not interested in conversation or negotiation - anyone entering the fort is treated as a threat to their employer.
Personality - The Dedicated Mercenary: Ulmug is a professional mercenary who takes his contract seriously. He maintains constant vigilance, patrolling without rest for eating or sleeping - a level of dedication that suggests either magical endurance or extreme discipline. He's hostile to all intruders, immediately treating them as threats to Roderick. He works cooperatively with his fellow mercenaries like Neesha, coordinating their movements and patrols. His positioning as the final guard before Roderick's medicine cabinet shows he's been assigned the most critical defensive position, suggesting trust in his abilities. Even after Roderick's fate is determined, Ulmug remains at his post indefinitely, still hostile - either unaware the contract is over or simply having nowhere else to go. He's a warrior doing his job with grim efficiency, unaware that forces beyond his ability to stop have already decided his employer's fate.
author: Bethesda
